All right, picking up where we left off, what we're gonna do is go to our order page. The reason that we have it set up this way so that you are logged in is so that it never logs, like if you do, if we had done affiliate links, you'd have to continue to click the link, which can be a pain, sometimes trainers can forget to do it and then things don't get attributed properly. In an earlier version, that is what we tried to do and it was a big failure. So we now have it, so it's hardwired this way, so and this has solved the problem and it's great and it's smooth and it's awesome. So let's buy something. There are three different, so you'll note that the prices here are listed are the website prices. This is the public facing prices, this is not this person is going to pay. So let's buy something. I would like to buy some, Burn AM, that is what I'm going to buy. So I'm gonna click there and then underneath you can see, it goes from one of seven to 67, which is awesome, it's a big price drop. Now, when you see this screen, there are three things that someone can do. They can either buy the item as it is with the distributor discount, which is still significant, and just click add to cart and they'll click anything else. That is how you just add it as a one-off item, it will not have recurring, it will not have any bulk discounts, it is just a one-off item that will get delivered to their home, all right. The next option is to go on subscribe and save, all right. Subscribe and save will knock this down to $59, okay. And it will put them on auto-ship so that every 30 days, they get a fresh bottle of whatever the product is. The third option is to get three, six, or 12 months of the item. Now, if someone is on six months, they're not going to then six months from now get another six months worth of stuff. It does not auto renew, there's no recurring component to it. What it is is it takes six bottles, puts them into a box and then ships it to their front door and they get a steep discount for buying in full upfront. So it's advantageous for customers, especially if they're buying like a single product to get three, six, or 12 months worth. And if someone is already committed to your facility or to work with you for six months or 12 months, then there's really no reason for them to pay more by getting a one-off. They should really get either subscribe and save if they can't afford the three, six, or 12. It's really, they should be doing one of these two things. It doesn't really make sense to do this one, especially if they're on a membership. But I'm a crazy person and I want to see what it's like and I want to walk you through it. So let's just buy one item. All right, so you'll notice that it's been added to the cart. We click over here. I'm gonna exit this one out because I was doing an earlier demonstration where I goofed up. And so you'll see this, this is now the distributor price. There are no discounts that have been applied to this besides the original distributor discount from 107 to 67. All right, and we're going to select expedited flat chipping which is what I recommend all the people do because they're not gonna want to wait a week and start working on all that kind of stuff. So this way they'll be able to get it by Thursday. And depending on when you're watching this video, if this is rolled out to all of legacy, then it is likely that they'll get it Monday to Wednesday because we will have sufficient volume to work two extra day full cruise in the warehouse to be able to get our stuff out even faster than that. But that is how this works. It is a flat rate, which also incentivizes them to buy more things. So proceed to check out. Now, one of the key notes here is that billing and shipping address are especially important. On your contracts, it doesn't matter as much for your gym, but for shipping physical product, shipping addresses are very important. We wanna make sure that we don't send it to their card address, which might be in a different state or something like that, or register to their parents' house. We wanna make sure that it's to the right address, all right? And so I'm actually going to put in my card right now, and we're going to do this together, all right? United States. And we're going to put our numbers in. Now, mind you, this is going to be the information of the consumer, not your information. Just worth saying. All right. And I'm not gonna click ship to a different address because my billing address is the exact same as my shipping address, all right? But if you were, you would check this box and then it would open up what the shipping address should be, all right? Now, here we are. Once we entered it in, you'll notice that the shipping cost populates again. It has the same options. Again, you don't need to change anything, but sometimes if you look over here earlier before you put your address in, it will not have populated. So don't forget about that. Now, put this in. And we will place this order. All right. Then they will get an order. You'll see this page, which is the thank you page. It'll pause there and then it'll automatically load you back up to order page so that you can complete the next transaction, especially if you have a stack of sales that you have to get through, et cetera. All right. That is how you do a one-off transaction. You can do a one-off transaction. You can do a one-off transaction. You can do a one-off transaction. You can do a one-off transaction.
